summary: Fixed GitHub Actions workflow plumbing for Melee builds by passing the matrix-selected MAKE_FLAGS into the Docker container instead of hard-coding NON_MATCHING=1, standardizing the output directory through an OUTPUT environment variable, and making artifact uploads fail when expected files are missing.
what_changed: Added an OUTPUT environment variable in build-melee.yml set to `${{ github.workspace }}/output`.
Replaced a local shell `output` variable with the workflow-level `$OUTPUT` path for directory creation, Docker volume mounting, artifact upload, and PROGRESS.md summary reading.
Changed the Docker invocation in build-melee.yml from hard-coded `--env MAKE_FLAGS="NON_MATCHING=1"` to `--env MAKE_FLAGS="$MAKE_FLAGS"`, allowing the matrix value to control the build.
Kept the matrix values `GENERATE_MAP=1` and `NON_MATCHING=1` but made the Docker build actually receive those values through the `MAKE_FLAGS` environment variable.
Changed the GALE01.map artifact path from `/tmp/output/ssbm.us.1.2/GALE01.map` to `${{ env.OUTPUT }}/ssbm.us.1.2/GALE01.map`.
Added `if-no-files-found: error` to the GALE01.map upload in build-melee.yml.
Added `if-no-files-found: error` to artifact uploads in dump-asm.yml and publish-pages.yml.
smart_moves: Moved output path handling into a job-level environment variable so all workflow steps refer to the same directory consistently.
Used the existing matrix value via `MAKE_FLAGS: ${{ matrix.make_flags }}` and passed it through to Docker, preserving the intended CI matrix behavior.
Made missing artifacts a hard CI error, which prevents workflows from appearing successful when map, dump, or pages output was not actually produced.
Updated both producer and consumer paths for output files, avoiding mismatch between mounted Docker output and later artifact/summary steps.
decomp_lessons: CI workflows that validate matching, maps, non-matching builds, or generated pages should fail loudly when expected artifacts are absent.
When a GitHub Actions matrix defines build flags, confirm those flags are actually passed into the command or container performing the build.
Prefer a single environment variable for shared output directories across Docker volume mounts, artifact uploads, and summary generation.
For decomp progress automation, path mismatches can silently break reporting such as GALE01.map uploads or PROGRESS.md summaries even when the build command itself runs.
naming_conventions: Uses uppercase workflow environment variables such as `OUTPUT`, `REGISTRY`, `IMAGE`, and `MAKE_FLAGS`.
Matrix key remains `make_flags`, while the shell/container environment variable is `MAKE_FLAGS`.
Artifact names remain descriptive and stable: `GALE01.map`, `dump`, and `pages`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No direct source matching or assembly tactics were changed.
The workflow fix supports matching-related CI by ensuring `GENERATE_MAP=1` and `NON_MATCHING=1` matrix builds are passed to Docker correctly.
The map-generation path now reads `${{ env.OUTPUT }}/ssbm.us.1.2/GALE01.map` and `$OUTPUT/PROGRESS.md`, which helps preserve automated progress reporting.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find workflow changes that add `if-no-files-found: error` to artifact uploads.
Find changes to GALE01.map artifact generation or upload paths.
Find GitHub Actions Docker volume mount fixes for output directories.
